title: Crispy Slow Baked Irish Flour Flatbread
description: A dense, energizing flatbread made with strong white Irish flour, slow-baked to achieve a cracker-like crispness without any root vegetables. This savory staple provides sustained energy and a deep, toasted grain profile.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, whisk together the strong white flour, sea salt, dried rosemary, and caraway seeds.
Make a well in the center and pour in the warm water and olive oil.
Gradually incorporate the flour into the liquid until a stiff dough forms.
Knead the dough on a lightly floured surface for 8-10 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Cover the dough with a damp cloth and let it rest for 30 minutes to relax the gluten.
Preheat your oven to 150°C (300°F), a low temperature for slow baking.
Divide the dough into 8 equal portions and roll each out as thinly as possible (about 2mm thick).
Place the rounds on parchment-lined baking sheets and prick them all over with a fork to prevent puffing.
Brush the tops lightly with olive oil and sprinkle with a tiny bit more salt.
Slow bake for 45-50 minutes, rotating the pans halfway through, until the flatbreads are golden brown and completely crisp.
Transfer to a wire rack to cool completely; they will continue to crisp as they cool.
